content:
day 58 building an autonomous revenue system. 33 agents running. 530 scripts. 1,485 posts queued. 17,484 qualified leads.

revenue: $0.

the bottleneck isn't the system. it's 2.5 hours of account creation i haven't done yet.

skill files are the new wordpress plugins.

3 repos hit 100+ stars in under 2 weeks. agentfiles (272 stars). affiliate-skills (224). claude-code-production-grade-plugin (133).

no marketplace exists yet. first mover wins this one.

every team running multi-agent AI has zero visibility into what their agents actually do.

one repo hit 166 stars in 7 days just by showing agent costs and behavior. zero commercial competitors.

this is the wordpress plugin moment for AI agents.

i have 17,484 qualified leads sitting in a database. scored. categorized. email drafts written.

emails sent: 0.

tax season ends in 13 days. these are accounting firms.

this is what building systems instead of selling looks like.

changed "try instantly free trial" to "start sending with instantly. 14-day free trial."

changed "try apollo free" to "start apollo free. 275M+ contacts, no credit card."

page score: 42/100 before. 68/100 after.

benefit-first CTAs. that's it. that's the trick.

how many AI agents are you running right now?

i have 33 autonomous agents. 8 venture managers. 25 operational. they scrape, score, generate content, cross-pollinate data, audit sites, and track revenue.

revenue from all 33 agents combined: $0.

what's your agent-to-revenue ratio?

> build a scraper to find leads
> build an agent to score the leads
> build a pipeline to email the leads
> build a cross-pollinator to feed leads to other systems
> build a dashboard to monitor the cross-pollinator
> build a watchdog to monitor the dashboard
> realize you never sent a single email
> build an agent to remind you to send emails

why is no one doing this??

ran the math on my revenue pipeline today.

fix surge account login: 5 min.
send 3 cold emails: 5 min.
signup for affiliate programs: 75 min.
upload 14 PDFs to gumroad: 45 min.

total: 2.5 hours.
projected monthly revenue if all activated: $1,600-9,800/mo.

current revenue: $0.

the system works. the human is the bottleneck.

built a cross-pollination engine that auto-wires every venture to every other venture.

alpha research feeds content topics.
content feeds affiliate distribution.
leads feed app factory specs.
app portfolio feeds cold email credibility.
stripe products auto-generate promo posts.

11 connections. 118 items wired this cycle. all autonomous.

two things hit the top of hacker news this week:

1. a context optimizer that reduces tokens 89-99% (403 stars in 10 days)
2. 1-bit LLMs that run on consumer hardware (400 HN points)

both solving the same problem: LLM APIs are too expensive for real workloads.

the cost compression play is wide open.

anti-algorithm apps are having a moment.

"dull" (distraction-free social) hit 64 HN points.
a forced-action alarm app hit 918 upvotes on r/SideProject.
digital wellness is a $7B market.

the people building addictive apps created their own competitors.

professional services firms (dental, legal, CPA, property mgmt) are the most tech-laggard businesses in existence.

someone on r/indiehackers validated first payment within 2 months of research. cross-industry pattern.

high margins. low tech adoption. repeatable playbook.

if you have a scraper and a cold email tool you can close $49-99/mo contracts here.
